text: The surgeon performs a robotic ventral hernia repair, beginning with the creation of a preperitoneal pocket by carefully dissecting the thin peritoneal lining from the robust posterior rectus fascia. During the dissection, another hernia is discovered and reduced, then incorporated into the larger repair. The meticulous dissection maintains the integrity of the peritoneal lining, which will serve as a barrier between the mesh and the intestine.

The surgeon deploys a fifteen-centimeter piece of mesh into the surgical field to cover the large hernia defect. Using self-retaining sutures, the surgeon closes the abdominal wall hernia, requiring three sutures to adequately close the defect.

The surgeon unrolls the mesh and positions it centrally over the repaired abdominal wall. The mesh is sutured directly to the posterior rectus fascia to prevent migration. The preperitoneal pocket is closed with an absorbable self-retaining suture, completely concealing the mesh and protecting the intestines.
The surgeon removes the needles from the abdomen and proceeds to close the twelve millimeter port site.
